raw_text: {"assignments": [{"slot_position": 1, "candidate_id": "clip-A", "rationale": "hook_score 9.2 — highest in pool, action-verb description (slams), fits hook role"}, {"slot_position": 2, "candidate_id": "clip-D", "rationale": "energy 4.5 within \u00b11.5 of slot target 5.0; description (dealer shuffles) breaks up the two reaction peaks at slots 1 and 3"}, {"slot_position": 3, "candidate_id": "clip-C", "rationale": "energy 9.0 matches slot target 9.0; description (leaps, fists raised) names a clear payoff beat for the punchline role"}]}

assignments.rationale: hook_score 9.2 — highest in pool, action-verb description (slams), fits hook role
energy 4.5 within ±1.5 of slot target 5.0; description (dealer shuffles) breaks up the two reaction peaks at slots 1 and 3
energy 9.0 matches slot target 9.0; description (leaps, fists raised) names a clear payoff beat for the punchline role

meta.notes: Hook→broll→punchline alternation. The 'wide casino floor' clip (B) is intentionally LEFT UNUSED — it's a defensible alternative for slot 2 but D's description fits better against the human action beats. The strict 'highest hook_score → hook slot' rule is also tested here (clip-A wins slot 1 over clip-C despite C's higher energy).
